message:
perf(raptorq): move RHS data instead of cloning during dense matrix construction

Replace state.rhs[eq_idx].clone() with std::mem::take(&mut state.rhs[eq_idx])
in both dense_gaussian_eliminate and dense_solve_remaining. When building
the dense matrix for Gaussian elimination, the RHS vectors (which are
symbol-sized byte buffers) were being cloned even though the source
equations are no longer needed after matrix construction.

Using mem::take moves the Vec<u8> out of the equation in O(1) instead
of cloning the full symbol data in O(symbol_size), eliminating
O(n_rows * symbol_size) bytes of heap allocation on the hot decode path.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
